summary: Plan has 4 blocking issues: 1 incorrect function signature, 1 crash on XML comments/PIs, 1 missing security documentation, 1 unbounded MemoryError failure path. B4 (normalization collision) resolved by schema-mode-dependent behavior: FIXED crashes (correct), OBSERVED/FLEXIBLE quarantines per-record (spec updated). 8 warnings should be addressed, most notably BOM handling, string typing documentation, and silent data drops without audit.

blocking_issues.issue: `create_schema_from_config()` called with wrong signature — missing `config` and `name` arguments
blocking_issues.evidence: Plan line 144: `create_schema_from_config(allow_coercion=True)`. Actual signature at schema_factory.py:87: `(config, name, allow_coercion)`

blocking_issues.issue: XML comment and PI nodes crash `_strip_namespace()` — `.tag` is callable, not string
blocking_issues.evidence: `ET.Comment('text').tag` returns `<built-in function Comment>`, not a string. `_strip_namespace()` calls `.startswith()` on it.
blocking_issues.resolution: Filter child elements with `isinstance(child.tag, str)` before processing. Add extraction rule and test case.

blocking_issues.issue: XML security posture (XXE, billion laughs, DTD) not documented
blocking_issues.evidence: Peer Dataverse spec documents this. XML source spec has no security section despite accepting untrusted XML input (Tier 3).
blocking_issues.resolution: Add Security Considerations section documenting Python 3.12+ expat defaults, entity expansion limits, and `defusedxml` decision.

blocking_issues.issue: Per-record normalization collision raises `ValueError` mid-stream, leaving partial run
blocking_issues.evidence: Plan line 164: collision raises ValueError. Unlike CSV (header-level = clean fail), XML collision at record 500 leaves 499 committed rows.
blocking_issues.resolution: Schema-mode-dependent behavior: FIXED schema crashes (correct — source cannot uphold field contract), OBSERVED/FLEXIBLE quarantines per-record (collision is local to that record, others may be well-formed). Spec updated with this distinction in load() step 6d, error handling table, and test cases.

blocking_issues.issue: `MemoryError` from large XML files has no bounded failure path — run left in open state
blocking_issues.evidence: `ET.parse()` loads entire DOM. MemoryError propagates as unhandled plugin crash with no terminal audit event.
blocking_issues.resolution: Document memory model in Operational Constraints section. Catch MemoryError in load(), emit audit error, propagate cleanly.

warnings.issue: BOM handling unspecified — UTF-8 BOM files common from Windows tools
warnings.recommendation: Verify ET.parse() BOM behavior, document, and add test case
Document .text vs grandchild interaction for mixed content

warnings.issue: All-strings typing not surfaced to pipeline authors — silent wrong gate routing risk
warnings.recommendation: Add documentation warning about string comparison in gates

warnings.issue: FieldResolution storage uses plain dict instead of FieldResolution dataclass
Grandchild element silent drop has no audit trail entry
warnings.recommendation: Add ctx.record_validation_error(..., destination='discard') per CSVSource precedent

warnings.issue: Attribute-child name collision is silent data loss (no audit of dropped attribute)
warnings.recommendation: Record collision in audit trail metadata

warnings.issue: No integration or E2E tests specified
warnings.recommendation: Add at least 1 integration test with full pipeline

warnings.issue: get_field_resolution() returns None before load() — no guard documented
warnings.recommendation: Document precondition or add state check

recommendations.suggestion: Import NORMALIZATION_ALGORITHM_VERSION from field_normalization.py rather than hard-coding
Add 14 missing test cases (BOM, mixed content, CDATA, comments, PIs, large files, empty file, etc.)
Document OBSERVED mode fragility for heterogeneous XML (first-row oracle may not represent full field space)
Normalization API divergence from CSV resolve_field_names() is acceptable — XML structure justifies different code path

conflicts_resolved.systems_view: Blocking — qualitatively different from CSV (per-record vs per-header)
conflicts_resolved.user_clarification: Schema contracts are DAG-level guarantees. FIXED schema means source promised specific fields — collision is a schema integrity failure (crash). OBSERVED/FLEXIBLE schema means contract is emergent — collision is per-record data quality issue (quarantine).
conflicts_resolved.resolution: RESOLVED. Spec updated with schema-mode-dependent behavior. B4 no longer blocking — the nuanced approach addresses both the systems concern (no partial runs in OBSERVED mode) and the contract integrity concern (FIXED mode still crashes cleanly).
Downgraded to warning. ET.parse() handles UTF-8 BOM in most configurations. Needs verification and documentation.

conflicts_resolved.systems_view: Blocking — no terminal audit event on MemoryError
conflicts_resolved.resolution: Systems view prevails. Ghost runs with no terminal event violate audit completeness. Classified as blocking (B5).
